description: Comprehensive test suite with 30+ tests covering the universal control layer: intent parsing, multi-step planning from natural language, workflow execution with rollback and confirmation, cross-app orchestration, voice-to-intent bridge, and the Ask Quant UI integration. Tests should exercise real code paths and would fail if the implementations were reverted.
steps: Step 1: In packages/quant-tools/src/__tests__/intent-parser.test.ts, write 8+ tests: (a) parse single-intent input 'send an email to bob@test.com', (b) parse multi-intent 'schedule meeting tomorrow at 5pm and message the team on chat', (c) extract temporal expressions correctly (tomorrow, at 5pm, next Monday at 3pm), (d) extract entity references (names, email addresses, team names), (e) handle ambiguous input gracefully, (f) return empty intents for garbage input, (g) resolve 'them'/'it' pronouns using context, (h) handle time zones in temporal expressions.

Step 2: In packages/quant-tools/src/__tests__/workflow-executor.test.ts, write 10+ tests: (a) execute single step successfully, (b) execute multi-step with dependencies passing data, (c) rollback on mid-workflow failure (verify undo handlers called in reverse order), (d) parallel execution of independent steps (verify they complete concurrently), (e) confirmation gate pauses execution and resumes on confirm, (f) confirmation gate rejects and stops execution on deny, (g) step timeout fires and marks step as failed, (h) retry on transient failure with backoff, (i) skip rollback when enableRollback is false, (j) audit trail records all actions including rollbacks.

Step 3: In packages/quant-tools/src/__tests__/universal-control-layer.test.ts, write 8+ integration tests: (a) full NL pipeline: 'send email to alice@test.com about the project' produces correct plan and execution, (b) multi-app NL: 'create a meeting tomorrow and send a chat message to the team' hits both calendar and chat tools, (c) context-aware NL: with currentItem set, 'forward this to bob' resolves correctly, (d) voice bridge test: mock STT transcription -> orchestrator produces results, (e) dry run mode shows plan without executing, (f) error in one step triggers rollback of previous steps, (g) permission check blocks high-tier actions for low-tier users, (h) concurrent workflow steps execute in parallel.

Step 4: In packages/ai/src/__tests__/voice-intent-bridge.test.ts, write 5+ tests: (a) processVoiceCommand transcribes then executes, (b) returns transcript alongside results, (c) handles STT failure gracefully, (d) passes userId/sessionId through correctly, (e) emits progress events during execution.

Step 5: In apps/quantai/src/__tests__/ask-quant-enhanced.test.tsx, write 5+ tests for the enhanced UI: (a) AskQuantPanel renders voice toggle and plan preview section, (b) confirmation dialog renders when confirmation_required event arrives, (c) rollback progress displays during rollback events, (d) voice recording integration sends audio data correctly, (e) plan preview shows before execution with execute button.
acceptance_criteria: At least 36 new tests total across all test files
All tests pass with pnpm --filter @quant/quant-tools test && pnpm --filter @quant/ai test && pnpm --filter @quant/quantai test
Tests exercise real code paths (IntentParser, WorkflowExecutor, CrossAppOrchestrator.processNaturalLanguage, VoiceIntentBridge)
Tests would fail if the corresponding implementation code were removed/reverted
No test uses only static assertions or mocks away all real logic
